SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

umm.digiquilt.model
Class Block

java.lang.Object
  umm.digiquilt.model.Block
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<Patch>
public class Block
extends Object
implements Iterable<Patch>

The block is the main work area for the program. This is the model representation, which stores the actual Patches and can swap and replace colors and patches, as well as calculating the fractions for colors.

Version:
$Revision: 1.3 $
Author:
Main.BrianOhs and Main.AndyMitchell, last changed by $Author: fortunan $ on $Date: 2009-07-01 23:35:06 $

          Default constructor.
Block(int size)
          Constructor that takes an int to determine the size of the Block (how many patches will be in the block).
 
Method Summary

          Method to clear the block and return it to new block status

          Equals method for Blocks.
 Block getBlockClone()
          This method returns a block identical to the block which the method was called upon, but it is a completely independent object.

          Method to get the Fraction that represents the coverage of the desired Fabric.

          Method to get a Patch at a specific location

          Method to get the height/width of this block, in patches
 int getSize()
          Method to get the size of the Block (in patches)
 int hashCode()
          Since we are overriding equals, we should implement our own hashcode.
 Iterator<Patch> iterator()
           
 void replaceFabricInBlock(Fabric replaceThis, Fabric withThis)
          Replaces fabrics in the block and then redraws the blockwork panel

          Goes through and replaces all instances of replaceThis with withThis.

          Method to put a patch on a specified location on the block, while checking that the specified location is valid.
 void swapFabricInBlock(Fabric one, Fabric two)
          This is the method to change one fabric in the backend to another fabric in the backend swapping both
 void swapPatchesInBlock(Patch swapThis, Patch andThis)
          replaces all instances of one patch with the other and vice versa
 
Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object
clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait
 

Constructor Detail

Block

public Block()
Default constructor. The default size should be SIXTEEN. Sets all patches to the default (gray) patch.

Block

public Block(int size)
Constructor that takes an int to determine the size of the Block (how many patches will be in the block).

Parameters:
size - The size to construct the Block with.
Method Detail

getSize

public int getSize()
Method to get the size of the Block (in patches)

Returns:
size The size of the Block.

getSideSize

public int getSideSize()
Method to get the height/width of this block, in patches

Returns:
height/width of block

setPatch

public void setPatch(Patch aPatch,
                     int location)
Method to put a patch on a specified location on the block, while checking that the specified location is valid.

Parameters:
aPatch - The patch to be placed.
location - the desired location to place the patch.

getPatch

public Patch getPatch(int location)
Method to get a Patch at a specific location

Parameters:
location - The location of the desired Patch.
Returns:
patchArray[location] The desired Patch.

equals

public boolean equals(Object element)
Equals method for Blocks.

Overrides:
equals in class Object
Returns:
true if the given block is equal to this one.

hashCode

public int hashCode()
Since we are overriding equals, we should implement our own hashcode.

Overrides:
hashCode in class Object
Returns:
int containing the hash

replaceFabricInBlock

public void replaceFabricInBlock(Fabric replaceThis,
                                 Fabric withThis)
Replaces fabrics in the block and then redraws the blockwork panel

Parameters:
replaceThis - the fabric to be replaced
withThis - the fabric to replace "replaceThis" with

swapFabricInBlock

public void swapFabricInBlock(Fabric one,
                              Fabric two)
This is the method to change one fabric in the backend to another fabric in the backend swapping both

Parameters:
one - the fabric to replace two
two - the fabric to replace one

getBlockClone

public Block getBlockClone()
This method returns a block identical to the block which the method was called upon, but it is a completely independent object.

Returns:
a clone of the block which it was called upon.

replacePatchInBlock

public void replacePatchInBlock(Patch replaceThis,
                                Patch withThis)
Goes through and replaces all instances of replaceThis with withThis. This method is the backend for the patch swap functionality of the Patch Work Area triangle

Parameters:
replaceThis - replace all instances of this patch in the block work area
withThis - this is the patch that replaces instances of the replaceThis

swapPatchesInBlock

public void swapPatchesInBlock(Patch swapThis,
                               Patch andThis)
replaces all instances of one patch with the other and vice versa

Parameters:
swapThis - one of the patches that is being swapped
andThis - the second patch that is being swapped

clear

public void clear()
Method to clear the block and return it to new block status

getBlockCoverage

public Fraction getBlockCoverage(Fabric fabric)
Method to get the Fraction that represents the coverage of the desired Fabric.

Parameters:
fabric - the fabric to get coverage of
Returns:
the fraction that this fabric covers

allTiles

public Iterable<Fabric> allTiles()
Returns:
an iteration over all of the tiles of this Block

iterator

public Iterator<Patch> iterator()
Specified by:
iterator in interface Iterable<Patch>
